REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANDREW LINTON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL, RETURN THE MATTER TO THE CIVIL ACTIVE LIST, AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR MANDATORY OR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF ON THE MERITS PER CCP 473(B) (ALSO SEE IS BASINGER V. ROGERS & WELLS (1990) 220 CAL.APP.3RD 16, 269) ADDITIONALLY, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITH THE MOVING PAPERS DEPRIVING PLAINTIFF OF AN OPPORTUNITY TO ADDRESS IT IN OPPOSITION. MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside Dismissal, Return The Matter To The Civil Active List, And For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint [Mandatory And/Or Discretionary Relief Under Ccp 473(B)]
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Anthony Flores; The Flores Law Firm; 415-235-3652; appearing as counsel for defendant and moving party Andrew Linton.
Justin A. Goodman; Zacks & Freedman, PC; 415-956-8100; appearing as counsel for plaintiff 1429 Grant Ave, LLC.

Defendant contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW LINTON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL, RETURN THE MATTER TO THE CIVIL ACTIVE LIST, AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party fails to establish a basis for mandatory or discretionary relief on the merits per CCP 473(b) (also see is Basinger v. Rogers & Wells (1990) 220 Cal.App.3rd 16, 269) Additionally, moving party failed to provide a copy of the proposed cross-complaint with the moving papers depriving Plaintiff of an opportunity to address it in opposition. Defendant's contention that inclusion of the proposed cross-complaint with the reply papers satisfied the procedural requirements of CCP 473(b) is not supported by any authority. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial notice is granted.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Emily Husary, CSR #12148, calicsr@yahoo.com, 707-799-5993 (D501)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Justin Goodman, Esq. of Zacks Freedman & Patterson, Tel. No. 415.956.8100, appearing for the Plaintiff 1429 Grant Ave., LLC;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to this motion being heard by Judge Pro Tem James Fleming.

There being no other appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Plaintiff 1429 Grant Ave, Llc's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition with the modification as to the date/time/place of the deposition as follows: The motion to compel appearance at deposition and produce documents is granted. Mr. Coyle will appear for his deposition and produce the requested documents at the offices of Zacks, Feedman & Patterson, PC, located at 235 Montgomery Street, Suite 400, San Francisco, CA 94014 commencing on April 5, 2018 at 10:00 a.m which is included in the order hereon. Opposing parties contend only that the motion must be denied based on alleged non-compliance with California Rule of Court 3.1346. However, CRC 3.1346 has no application to disobedience to a subpoena which is present here, as opposed to refusal to answer questions or produce documents at a deposition. Moreover, that section provides no consequences for non-adherence to its terms. No authority has been cited that non-adherence requires denial of the motion. In any case, Mr. Coyle has had adequate notice of this hearing. Sanctions are denied but Mr. Coyle is informed that monetary sanctions are available to compel compliance with the Discovery Act and orders like this one.

Judge Pro Tem: James H. Fleming, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)
